title: DataLinq configuration
description: Configuration for the DataLinq CLI and model-generation workflow.

$schema.description: JSON Schema URL used by editors for autocomplete and validation.

Databases.description: Logical database configurations used by DataLinq CLI commands.

Name.description: Database entry name used by CLI selection through --database.

CsType.description: C# database model type name. Defaults to Name when omitted.

Namespace.description: C# namespace used for generated model declaration files. Defaults to Models when omitted.

ModelDirectory.description: Directory where DataLinq writes generated model declaration files and reads existing declaration files during regeneration.

DestinationDirectory.description: Deprecated compatibility alias for ModelDirectory. Prefer ModelDirectory in new config files.

SourceDirectories.description: Deprecated. Existing model declaration files are read from ModelDirectory instead of a separate source directory.

Include.description: Optional table or view names to include during generation.

UseRecord.description: Generate record-style model declarations. Defaults to false.

UseFileScopedNamespaces.description: Generate file-scoped namespace declarations. Defaults to false.

UseNullableReferenceTypes.description: Generate nullable reference annotations and #nullable enable in generated model files. Defaults to true.

CapitalizeNames.description: Capitalize generated C# names inferred from database names. Defaults to false.

RemoveInterfacePrefix.description: Remove leading interface prefixes when reading existing model declarations. Defaults to true.

SeparateTablesAndViews.description: Generate tables and views into separate files where supported. Defaults to false.

Connections.description: Provider connection entries. Prefer local or secret-bearing connection strings in datalinq.user.json rather than committed datalinq.json.

FileEncoding.description: Encoding used when reading and writing generated model declaration files.

PropertyOrder.description: Order scalar properties by database column order or C# property name.

KeyPlacement.description: Place primary-key scalar properties at the top or leave them inline with the selected property order.

RelationPlacement.description: Place relation properties at the bottom, top, or near their foreign-key scalar property group.

Type.description: Built-in database provider used by this connection.

DatabaseName.description: Compatibility alias for DataSourceName. Prefer DataSourceName in new config files.

DataSourceName.description: Logical database name, server-side database name, or SQLite file path depending on provider.

ConnectionString.description: Provider connection string. CLI commands support ${env:NAME}, ${secret:name}, and ${prompt:label} references. Prefer local values or secret references in datalinq.user.json.
